sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT PRIMARY KEY, event_date DATE, description VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date, description) VALUES (1, {d '2020-03-22'}, 'Event A');
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date, description) VALUES (2, {d '2021-01-01'}, 'Event B');
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date, description) VALUES (3, NULL, 'Event C');
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date, description) VALUES (4, {d '2023-12-31'}, 'Event D');
SELECT {fn DAYNAME(event_date)} AS dayname FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL AND {fn DAYNAME(event_date)} > 'Monday';
SELECT id, {fn DAYNAME(event_date)} AS dayname FROM test_dates WHERE event_date > {d '2020-01-01'};
SELECT t1.id, {fn DAYNAME(t1.event_date)} FROM test_dates t1 JOIN (SELECT {d '2020-03-22'} AS event_date) t2 ON t1.event_date > t2.event_date;
SELECT COUNT(id) FROM test_dates GROUP BY {fn DAYNAME(event_date)} HAVING COUNT(id) > 1;
SELECT id, (CASE WHEN event_date IS NULL THEN 'No Date' ELSE {fn DAYNAME(event_date)} END) AS dayname FROM test_dates;
SELECT t1.id FROM test_dates t1 LEFT JOIN test_dates t2 ON {fn DAYNAME(t1.event_date)} > {fn DAYNAME(t2.event_date)} WHERE t2.id IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT id, {fn DAYNAME(event_date)} AS dayname FROM test_dates) AS sub WHERE dayname IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id FROM test_dates WHERE event_date < {d '2021-06-01'} AND {fn DAYNAME(event_date)} > 'Friday';
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date, description) VALUES (5, {d '1900-01-01'}, 'Old Event');
SELECT id, event_date, {fn DAYNAME(event_date)} AS dayname FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL ORDER BY dayname;
UPDATE test_dates SET event_date = {d '2023-07-01'} WHERE {fn DAYNAME(event_date)} = 'Sunday';
DELETE FROM test_dates WHERE {fn DAYNAME(event_date)} = 'Saturday' AND id > 1;
